Description: An Advantage is a bonus that can be applied to a single roll. It may be a +1 bonus, a re-roll, or some other benefit.
Rules: Advantages can be gained through clever play, by using a Character's strengths, or by spending a resource such as a Fate Point.

Description: A Disadvantage is a penalty that can be applied to a single roll. It may be a -1 penalty, a forced re-roll, or some other hindrance.
Rules: Disadvantages can be imposed by the GM as a consequence of a Character's actions, or by an opponent's special ability.

Description: A Complication is a new problem or obstacle that arises during a scene. It may be a new enemy, a sudden change in the environment, or a moral dilemma.
Rules: Complications are used to increase the challenge and make the story more interesting. They should be a direct or indirect consequence of the Characters' actions.
